nodes.name: epic-comparison-page-bowl-playoff-support.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-COMPARISON-BOWL-PLAYOFF (Medium priority, Low complexity, Ready for Development). Enhances the prediction comparison page to display bowl and playoff game (weeks 16-20) ELO vs AP Poll accuracy alongside regular season data. Three stories: backend API extension to include postseason weeks with regular_season_accuracy/postseason_accuracy fields, frontend Chart.js update with descriptive postseason week labels (Bowl Week 1, CFP Quarterfinals, etc.), and a postseason statistics UI section. Additive changes only, no breaking changes to existing API.

nodes.name: epic-fix-ci-test-failures.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-FIX-CI-TESTS (High priority, Low-Medium complexity, Draft). Fixes 9 failing unit tests to achieve 100% pass rate (511/511). Three story groups: fix admin endpoint UnboundLocalError for UpdateTask import (4 tests), fix CFBD client get_current_week() returning None instead of correct week (2 tests), and consolidate weekly_update.py to call sys.exit() exactly once per error scenario (3 tests). Pure logic bug fixes with no API contract or schema changes.

nodes.name: epic-fix-playoff-games-COMPLETION.md
nodes.summary: Completion summary for EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES (completed 2025-12-21, ~4 hours). Documents achieved results: 8 playoff games imported for 2025 (4 first-round + 4 quarterfinal placeholders), week validation extended from 0-15 to 0-19 across all relevant files, API limit updated from 1,000 to 30,000 calls, and automated playoff import confirmed working for future seasons. Includes first-round game results with ELO changes (Alabama +43.2, Miami +41.3, Ole Miss +19.2, Oregon +19.9). Zero regressions on regular season imports.

nodes.name: epic-fix-playoff-games-import.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-FIX-PLAYOFF-GAMES (High priority, Low-Medium complexity, Complete). Defines the plan to fix missing/incomplete 2024 College Football Playoff game imports and implement sustainable playoff handling. Three stories: diagnose root cause of 0-0 placeholder scores, import all 8 playoff games (4 first-round + 4 quarterfinals) with correct data and TBD opponent handling, and enhance game import logic to automatically handle postseason games in future seasons. Uses existing CFBD API cfbd_client.py infrastructure.

nodes.name: epic-fix-remaining-int-tests.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-FIX-REMAINING-TESTS (High priority, Low-Medium complexity). Completes integration test suite stabilization by fixing 13 failing tests (104/117 passing to 100%). Story 1 fixes ModuleNotFoundError for cfbd_client import in admin endpoints (3 incorrect import paths in main.py, 5 tests). Story 2 fixes rankings/teams API tests that create Team objects but query RankingHistory table (post-EPIC-024 architecture change) by creating proper RankingHistory and Game records in test setup (8 tests). No production code changes except import path fixes.

nodes.name: epic-game-date-display-and-sorting.md
nodes.summary: EPIC-GAME-DATE-SORTING (Medium-High priority, Low complexity). Adds game date display and chronological secondary sorting (week then date) to improve UX, especially for bowl/playoff games spread across multiple days. Three stories: add date column to games.html UI, update /api/games and /api/teams/{id}/schedule API endpoints to sort by week then game_date (with null dates last), and enhance date display across team schedules and predictions with relative dates for upcoming games. Leverages existing game_date field — no schema changes needed.
